item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cool hook, but the unnecessary censorship at the end bothered me. I'm not allowed to know how big the damn thing is? Why not?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't have it any other way. Thanks for the response.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Tentative approval; I've added the crosslink, though reserve the ability to remove in the future.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edits have been made by author. Timer removed.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

It was proposed up thread that a potentially interesting mash up would be to release a bunch of SCP-588 instances upon SCP-1779. I'm not a dragon biologist, but I imagine that if an instance of SCP-1779 at a ton of carnivorous coins, there would be substantial internal damage done. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Currently, SCP-1779-08 has a length of [INSUFFICIENT CLEARANCE] and a weight of [INSUFFICIENT CLEARANCE]. Nesting rations are no longer being provided; at this time, SCP-1779-08 appears contented to remain inside Chamber 3 in East Building, and further containment procedures are not required. Personnel are to access Chamber 3 only when absolutely required.

To this:

Currently, SCP-1779-08 has a length of [INSUFFICIENT CLEARANCE] and a weight of [INSUFFICIENT CLEARANCE]. Nesting rations are no longer being provided; at this time, SCP-1779-08 appears contented to remain inside Chamber 3 in East Building, and further containment procedures are not required. Personnel are to access Chamber 3 only when absolutely required. Controlled use of SCP-588 is currently being investigated as a means of termination should SCP-1779-08 attempt to breach containment.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, it's a goddamned dragon - nice :D

Caught that about halfway through the article :)

item.title: There's the budget.
item.content:encoded:
It seems as though the composition of the coins matters more than their face value to these lizards. Is there any particular reason the researchers chose to specify "$ in assorted change," as opposed to "___ kg minted silver" or something similar?

That said, I like how the Foundation can literally pour millions of dollars into the -8 experiment without batting an eye.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still love this, and I realize it's kind of late in the game now, but on rereading it struck me as quite…off, I guess, that there's no mention of this things teeth. If they chew coins, I can't help but think the researchers would have something to say about what they use to do so beyond "strong jaws".

Chisel-shaped teeth? Flat molars for grinding? Maybe the enamel contains a lot of tungsten? Things like that, my inquiring mind wants to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, so they have made their own Smaug. +1 for the addendums.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I R luv this so mucho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THE DRGN HAS VOTED

(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate you for explaining this, because I can't upvote again for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really, really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I question the use of the excess, that's 3.5 million in gold and silver as well as 12 grand in change.

For truly wealthy people, this is chump change.

And it's for science.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I soooooooo want to throw this guy in a room with some SCP-588s and see who wins.

Or maybe they could work something out and peacefully coexist, even. I'll eat your back, you eat mine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, if you want to go after the dragon's hoard, feel free. I'll be standing over here with the emergency medical kit…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I couldn't find a way to properly include the first point into the article, but the reason they only eat money is because it actually has monetary worth. Toss blanks at them and they'll look at you as if you were an idiot and wait for actual coins. It's not a particularly scientific concept, but they actually strictly feed on cash.

And the reason that Puff got bigger and bigger is because they fed him gold and silver. I wasn't sure how to include THIS part, either, because it's also said that we don't know how they have metallic skeletons (and thus don't know exactly how their metabolism works. Basically, 1779's bones are susceptible to oxidation and rust. That's why

Waste produced by SCP-1779 consists of dull metallic dust, which increases or reduces in frequency and amount depending on the individual's diet.

Their skeletons rust. They poop the rust. If their skeletons don't rust or corrode, they don't poop, and the metals remain in their system indefinitely.

I am not a chemist or biologist, and this isn't particularly scientific. But by God i just busted a dragon out of a parking meter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really like this, particularly the parking meter specimen.

But why just coins? Sure, coins are a common source of fairly high-grade metals, and they come in convenient bite-sizes for tiny dragonlets. But don't you think a metalvore would gnaw on a lamp post if that was all the food readily available? And what about the experimental specimen? Surely a blankity-blank meter long beasty like him would prefer a nice mouthful of steel girder to the relatively piddling crumbs a coin represents.

More open-ended feeding habits could also make the test log more interesting, as how do you make a cage big/strong enough to hold 'Puff' without using metal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was also one of my favorite elements of the post. I have to echo that the off-standard phrasing for redacted phrases did nag at me a little, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thiiiird one. I'm going to see if I can make it clearer.

EDIT : Is that alright?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a third log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no fewer than twelve (16, or $4)

Nope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Biologically speaking, SCP-1779 are unique in that their skeletal systems will utilize metals extracted from common currency in the place of most other materials, forming novel alloys depending on their diet.

Either:

1) Its entire body is made from metal, not just its skeleton, and its entire biology is novel/anomalous.

2) The non-bone parts of its body appear to be the same as that of non-anomalous Earth animals, and the mass of those parts and the energy to run them come from eating plant and animal matter.

3) The non-bone parts of its body appear to be the same as that of non-anomalous Earth animals, and the mass of those parts and the energy to run them have an anomalous source, like transmuting the metal of the coins into other materials.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took me a while to find this, but it fits:

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ON-7v4qnHP8

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adorable parking meter dragons. This and the dragon snails make me enjoy dragons once again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this! I actually didn't get that it was a dragon until it started sleeping curled around its horde. Too bad it doesn't breathe fire; I gave a +1 by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I'm having a strong sense of deja-vu while reading your post. I don't know why though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't question the funding, I question the use of the excess, that's 3.5 million in gold and silver as well as 12 grand in change. What doi they use this money on? A bed for an overgrown penny munching reptile. Surely they could use that money on other projects or for other things, but instead it's left to the dragon.

It needs a justification or else it's just a butt load of money that is essentially wasted, it really reminds me of that one bad humanoid whose cell door was made of gold and diamonds for no reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed. And Bio-Site 66 has a crapton of extra funding, seeing as most of its other items are relatively cheap to contain.1

Footnotes
1. I know nothing of the funding of large organizations, and am aware that this particular statement is inaccurate as to how an actual organization would work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
whose diet consists exclusively of metal currency

and then…

Instead, they feed exclusively upon metallic currency

That aside this bit really bothers me:

SCP-1779-08's nest is estimated to have a worth of approximately $12 000 in change, and $3.5 million, possibly more, in gold and silver.

Really? Why? We can feed these things on pennies and yet this one has a bed worth more than a fancy house? Couldn't we use that money on something more important? It feels like a gross misuse of Foundation resources.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
noooooo, iwas gonna use that image

Aparently, you had the same idea as me, but i think you worked with this even more of what i expected. upvoted :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I like it. Though I was wondering when we'd get a dragon SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Math things aside, I rather like the quirkiness of this one, with a heaping helping of "Well, they don't get much bigger than such and such in the wild, we may as well see what happens, OH FUCK IT'S HUGE"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 12/4=3 12 quarters is not 4$ 16 quarters on the other hand is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good elaboration off that pic (I loved it when I saw it in the inspiration page). I also like the growing change in the Foundation's assumptions from "Oh look, it's a mini modern dragonette, almost like it's a real dragon" to "Oh shit, no it's an actual classical Western dragon. The others were just underfed"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Skyrim theme starts playing*

FUS-RO-DAH!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I already said on the photo page how much I love this picture, and the article is definitely good enough to support it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that the written logs outnumber the unwritten logs satisfies my complaint, downvote removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a fan of using .x meters measurements instead of simply using centimeters, but that's no reason not to upvote this. Nice work. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I was originally going for, TBH.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally like them. Gives a nice 'oh shit' feeling to round off the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really like the concept of putting an incomplete article up, but I suppose different strokes for different folks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about to upvote this, but then I saw those two redacted logs. You should know better Dex…downvoted for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And so does the article, now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the ability to move at speeds up to 52 km/h (~40 miles/h) in short bursts

Um, the Internet says 52 km/h is about 32 miles/h.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I probably will, once they are clearer in my head. Kind of like how I wrote 517s log a solid 2 weeks or more after the article came out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why don't you just write them out, then? I mean, you have it in your head, I don't see the purpose of redacting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like the idea behind this, and the execution is good, so +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, in my mind, in the middle of year two they realized "wait this thing isn't going to stop growing if we keep feeding it." and now they have a 70 foot greedy Wolverine dragon holed up in one of their buildings. I just don't like the look of [REDACTED UNDER O5 COMMAND] or [REDACTED UNDER LEVEL 4 CLEARANCE] because it bugs me.

But cool, if that's your only nitpick. i'm fixing the other things you mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Specimens of SCP-1779 generally grow to an average length of .4 metres long
On average, a wild instance of SCP-1779 will reach approximately 1 metre in length

I'm confused.

SCP-1779 are capable of breeding if they have a length of .5 metres or more.

So, the specimen has to be above-average size to breed? That doesn't make much sense to me…?

I don't really see the point of the 'insufficient clearance' aspects of the object. I don't know why they're there if we can't read them.

Neutral vote, since the insufficient clearances bother me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man I can't money. 75 nickels is not 15 dollars, Dex.

Thanks, Tox, for catching that.
